title: Listening - Part 2
description: For each question, choose the correct answer.
instructions: You will hear six short extracts. For questions 8–13, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear.

extracts.context: You hear two people talking about a lost bag.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Did you find your sports bag after football practice? || Man: Yes, but not where I expected. I checked the changing room first. || Woman: Was it still by the pitch? || Man: No. [8]Someone had taken it to the school office because my name was on the side[/8]. I was lucky, really. My trainers were inside. I suppose it is a small change, but it makes the situation much easier. That should make the plan easier and help everyone know what to expect.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Did you find your sports bag after football practice?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Yes, but not where I expected. I checked the changing room first.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Was it still by the pitch?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: No. Someone had taken it to the school office because my name was on the side. I was lucky, really. My trainers were inside. I suppose it is a small change, but it makes the situation much easier. That should make the plan easier and help everyone know what to expect.

questions.question: Where was the man's sports bag?
questions.explanation: The man says someone had taken the bag to the school office.

extracts.context: You hear a woman talking to a friend about a concert.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Are you still going to the concert on Friday? || Woman: Yes, but we're not driving now. Parking near the theatre is expensive. || Man: The bus takes ages from your house. || Woman: True, so [9]we're taking the train and walking from the station[/9]. It only takes ten minutes, and we won't have to worry about traffic afterwards. I think that is the most sensible choice after looking at the other possibilities. It means there should be fewer problems when the time comes to do it.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Are you still going to the concert on Friday?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes, but we're not driving now. Parking near the theatre is expensive.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: The bus takes ages from your house.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: True, so we're taking the train and walking from the station. It only takes ten minutes, and we won't have to worry about traffic afterwards. I think that is the most sensible choice after looking at the other possibilities. It means there should be fewer problems when the time comes to do it.

questions.question: How will the woman travel to the concert?
questions.explanation: The woman says they are taking the train and walking from the station.

extracts.context: You hear two friends talking about a new neighbour.
extracts.audio_script: Boy: Have you met the family who moved into number twelve? || Girl: Only the daughter. She came to borrow a ladder yesterday. || Boy: What is she like? || Girl: [10]She seems very friendly, although she was worried about making noise while they unpacked[/10]. I told her nobody would mind during the day. I would rather decide now than leave everything until the last minute. That way, we can enjoy it more and avoid rushing around later.

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: Have you met the family who moved into number twelve?

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: Only the daughter. She came to borrow a ladder yesterday.

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: What is she like?

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: She seems very friendly, although she was worried about making noise while they unpacked. I told her nobody would mind during the day. I would rather decide now than leave everything until the last minute. That way, we can enjoy it more and avoid rushing around later.

questions.question: What does the girl say about the new neighbour?

options.B: She is difficult to talk to.

questions.explanation: The girl says the new neighbour seems very friendly but was worried about making noise.

extracts.context: You hear a teacher giving information about a test.
extracts.audio_script: Teacher: Before you leave, please listen carefully about tomorrow's English test. It will not be in our usual classroom. || Teacher: The library is being used for exams, so don't go there either. || Teacher: [11]You need to meet outside Room 14 at nine o'clock[/11]. Bring a pencil and a rubber, but dictionaries are not allowed. It is not perfect, but it seems better than the other choices we had. The important thing is to keep it simple and not make extra work.

dialogue.speaker: teacher
dialogue.text: Before you leave, please listen carefully about tomorrow's English test. It will not be in our usual classroom.

dialogue.speaker: teacher
dialogue.text: The library is being used for exams, so don't go there either.

dialogue.speaker: teacher
dialogue.text: You need to meet outside Room 14 at nine o'clock. Bring a pencil and a rubber, but dictionaries are not allowed. It is not perfect, but it seems better than the other choices we had. The important thing is to keep it simple and not make extra work.

questions.question: Where should the students go for the test?
questions.explanation: The teacher says students need to meet outside Room 14.

extracts.context: You hear two friends discussing a camping trip.
extracts.audio_script: Man: I bought a new tent for the camping trip. It was cheap, but I hope it's strong enough. || Woman: Mine is easy to put up, but it's heavy to carry. || Man: We only have a short walk from the bus stop. || Woman: Good. [12]I'm more worried about keeping warm at night than carrying the tent[/12]. The forecast says it may be cold, even in June. It should be fine as long as everybody remembers the new arrangement.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I bought a new tent for the camping trip. It was cheap, but I hope it's strong enough.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Mine is easy to put up, but it's heavy to carry.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: We only have a short walk from the bus stop.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Good. I'm more worried about keeping warm at night than carrying the tent. The forecast says it may be cold, even in June. It should be fine as long as everybody remembers the new arrangement.

questions.question: What is the woman mainly worried about?
questions.explanation: The woman says she is more worried about keeping warm at night.

extracts.context: You hear a woman talking about a photography course.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Did you learn a lot on your photography course? || Woman: Yes, but not what I expected. I thought we'd spend most of the day outdoors. || Man: Didn't you? || Woman: Only for an hour. [13]The best part was learning how to change photos on the computer afterwards[/13]. I didn't know small changes could make such a big difference. I am glad we checked the details before making the final decision. That gives us enough time to prepare without changing everything else.

dialogue.text: Did you learn a lot on your photography course?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes, but not what I expected. I thought we'd spend most of the day outdoors.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Didn't you?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Only for an hour. The best part was learning how to change photos on the computer afterwards. I didn't know small changes could make such a big difference. I am glad we checked the details before making the final decision. That gives us enough time to prepare without changing everything else.

questions.question: What did the woman like best about the course?
questions.explanation: The woman says the best part was learning how to change photos on the computer.
